LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) NO PROPOSED ORDER NOR PROPOSED JUDGMENT RECEIVED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE AND FOLLOW THE COURT'S ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND A PROPOSED JUDGMENT CONFORMING TO THE REQUIREMENTS IN THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE AND CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, IDEALLY ON THE APPROPRIATE JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM AND MUST DROP A HARD COURTESY COPY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2026 Continued To Jul-28-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties
Off Calendar Mar-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A.'s unopposed Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing and consistent with the parties' agreement and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, judgment shall be entered as such: Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A. shall recover $2,269.29 in principal and $318.49 in costs, less $150 in credit for previous payments, for a total judgment of $2,437.78. This is the judgment authorized by the settlement agreement.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above text verbatim.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court, ideally on the appropriate Judicial Council form.

No proposed order nor proposed judgment received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court, ideally on the appropriate Judicial Council form and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
